summary: PR 2678 performed a repository-wide canonical symbol rename from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel`, including the GALE01 symbol entry at `.sdata:0x804D4A08`, the `src/melee/db/db.h` extern, the definition in `src/melee/db/dbinit.c`, and uses across db, fighter, game-mode, menu, map, library, and toy/display code. The PR title says the rename was based on an assert, but the loaded slice does not include the assert text itself. Several touched files also show formatting-only churn around long expressions, includes, casts, and line wrapping.

key_files.role: Renamed global `.sdata` symbol at `0x804D4A08` from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel`.
Updated the public extern declaration from `extern int g_debugLevel;` to `extern int DbLevel;`.
Renamed the global definition `int g_debugLevel = 1;` to `int DbLevel = 1;` and updated db setup/frame checks.
Updated debug-level gate in object allocation limiter from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel`.
Updated panic/error handlers to pass `DbLevel` to `Exception_StoreDebugLevel`.
Updated item/pokemon debug menu gate from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel`.
Updated Peach appeal debug-level check to `DbLevel`.
Updated fighter input and NaN/assert debug-level checks to `DbLevel`.
Updated stale-move/debug-level guard to `DbLevel`.
Updated multiple game-mode debug input/result gates to `DbLevel`.
Updated main loop debug hooks such as screenshot/thread checks to `DbLevel`.
Updated on-frame debug-level gate to `DbLevel`.
Updated scene debug gate to `DbLevel`; also includes line-wrap formatting churn.
Large formatting/include/cast churn in event-mode code; no loaded excerpt shows `DbLevel` use in this file despite being in the changed-file list.

what_changed: Renamed the symbol-table entry `g_debugLevel = .sdata:0x804D4A08` to `DbLevel = .sdata:0x804D4A08` while preserving type, size, scope, and section metadata shown in the diff.
Renamed the db header extern from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel`.
Renamed the defining global in `src/melee/db/dbinit.c` from `int g_debugLevel = 1;` to `int DbLevel = 1;`.
Propagated the rename through debug-level conditionals and error/debug reporting calls across db, fighter, game-mode, menu, map, library, and toy/display files.
Included formatting-only or style churn in at least `src/melee/gm/gm_1B03.c` and `src/melee/gm/gm_1BA8.c`, such as line wrapping, include ordering, and cast spacing.
smart_moves: The rename was applied at the symbol metadata layer, the owning db header declaration, the definition, and call sites instead of introducing a compatibility alias.
The new name preserves the address and symbol metadata in `symbols.txt`, avoiding a data ownership or section-placement change.
The PR title records the claimed evidence source for the rename: an assert naming the symbol `DbLevel`.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For global data-symbol renames, update the canonical symbol entry, owning header extern, definition, and all uses together; do not leave duplicate aliases for the same address.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: multiple call-site hunks replacing `g_debugLevel` with `DbLevel`

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert-derived names can be useful naming evidence, but the direct assert text should be preserved or cited when making the rename rationale reviewable.
evidence_refs: PR title: Rename `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel` based on assert

naming_conventions.name: DbLevel
naming_conventions.scope: global int debug-level variable in db subsystem, symbol address `.sdata:0x804D4A08`
naming_conventions.notes: The loaded evidence supports that this PR adopted `DbLevel` as the canonical name. The basis being an assert is only visible in the PR title, not in a loaded assert string or source hunk.

diff_excerpt: dbinit.c definition remains initialized to `1`
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No build, objdiff, or regression output is included in the loaded slice, so no match result is claimed.

Search for remaining `g_debugLevel` after PR 2678 to confirm the mechanical rename was complete.
Review `src/melee/ty/toy.c` and unshown hunks in `gmmain.c`, `mnevent.c`, and other one-line files if exact per-file rename details are needed.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Canonical global renames should touch symbols, headers, definitions, and uses together
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2678 shows a clean global data-symbol rename pattern: the symbol-table name, owning header extern, defining global, and all visible uses were changed from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel` without adding an alias or changing address/type metadata.

source_update_candidates.title: `DbLevel` global debug-level symbol
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2678 renamed the global debug-level symbol at `.sdata:0x804D4A08` from `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel`; the header declaration is `extern int DbLevel;` in `src/melee/db/db.h`, and the definition is `int DbLevel = 1;` in `src/melee/db/dbinit.c`.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete symbol/path fact from the merged PR, but it should be curated into source-owned path facts rather than promoted by the intake agent.

source_update_candidates.title: Record assert-backed `DbLevel` naming evidence if assert source is found
source_update_candidates.text: The PR title says the rename to `DbLevel` was based on an assert. Curator may want to locate and cite the exact assert evidence before treating the assert as authoritative naming evidence in a data sheet.
evidence_refs: PR title: Rename `g_debugLevel` to `DbLevel` based on assert
source_update_candidates.reason: The intake slice does not include the assert text, so the assertion basis remains under-evidenced.

rejection_notes: Do not claim this PR proved any function match, build success, or objdiff result; no such evidence is loaded.
Do not treat the formatting churn in `gm_1BA8.c` or other files as a decomp tactic without additional evidence.
Do not promote the assert rationale as fully verified until the actual assert text/source is located.
